sound_type: high whine + growl -- high-pitched whine/scream (distress, separation), growls/grunts (aggression), soft coos (bonding). Pup cry loud and piercing. E=4
ecosystem: Kelp forests, nearshore rocky habitats, sheltered coastlines. Resident on Washington's outer coast. Slowly recolonizing.
season: Year-round resident. Does not migrate. Pupping year-round with peak in spring.
family: Mustelidae (RETURNS -- last at degrees 63-68 river otter)

coordinates.description: Sea Otter on Washington's Olympic Coast. Kelp forest habitat. Reintroduced population.

taxonomy_note.description: MUSTELIDAE RETURNS -- weasel family, last seen at degrees 63-68 (river otter). The smallest marine mammal. The only marine mammal that uses tools. Keystone species of kelp forest ecosystems. Densest fur of any animal.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Enhydra from Greek 'en' (in) + 'hydra' (water) = 'in water.' lutris from Latin 'lutra' (otter). 'Sea Otter' for its exclusively marine habitat.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Enhydra -- monotypic genus. Only species: E. lutris. No close living relatives.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Same order as Harbor Seal (180), Steller Sea Lion (182), California Sea Lion (183), Northern Fur Seal (184).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fifth marine mammal family in the engine: Phocidae (180-181), Otariidae (182-184), Mustelidae (185). Three families in 6 degrees.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era continues (180-185+). Six consecutive marine mammals.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 200-6000 Hz. Wide range reflecting varied repertoire.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Whine: 0.5-3 sec. Growl: 0.3-1.5 sec. Coo: continuous soft murmur.
song_metrics.note_structure: High whine (distress), growl (aggression), coo (bonding), pup cry (loud, piercing).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Variable. Whines repeated when distressed. Growls in short bursts. Coos continuous.
song_metrics.timbre: Whine: piercing, high. Growl: guttural, low. Coo: soft, warm. Pup cry: piercing, urgent.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Moderate average. Pup cry is loudest. Coos are softest. Varied and emotionally expressive.
song_metrics.tempo: Variable by context. Alarm calls rapid. Social sounds irregular.
song_metrics.context: Surface vocalizations (not underwater). Kelp beds, haul-outs, mother-pup groups.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Some population-level differences in call structure. Individual recognition between mother and pup.
song_metrics.key_property: HIGH WHINE + GROWL SURFACE. MUSTELIDAE RETURNS. Tool-user. Densest fur. Kelp keystone. E=4. v1.49.326.

pelage.overall: Dark brown to black. Dense, luxurious fur -- up to 1 million hairs per square inch. Two layers: waterproof guard hairs + dense insulating underfur. Head often lighter (graying with age).
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Dense dark fur. Light/gray face (older individuals). Rounded body floating on back. No external ears. Webbed hind feet. Forepaws with retractile claws.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Continuous molt (not seasonal). Fur maintained by constant grooming.
pelage.swimming_pattern: Swims on back (unique among marine mammals). Hind feet and tail for propulsion. Forepaws for grooming and tool use. Often floats in kelp beds.

physical_description.overall: Length: 1.0-1.5 m. Weight: 14-45 kg (smallest marine mammal). Long body. Webbed hind feet. Dexterous forepaws. Dense fur. No blubber.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Floating on back in kelp. Dense dark fur. Light face. Dexterous forepaws manipulating food or rocks. Smallest marine mammal.
physical_description.field_id: Small dark-furred mammal floating on back in kelp beds or nearshore. Often holding food or rock on belly. Light/gray face. Much smaller than seals or sea lions. Listen for high-pitched whine.

nodes.title: Tool Use -- Only Tool-Using Marine Mammal
nodes.description: Rocks as anvils on belly. Favorite rocks stored in skin pouches. True tool use: selecting, transporting, using objects. Forward planning. Object recognition. Unique among marine mammals.

nodes.title: Kelp Forest Keystone -- Trophic Cascade
nodes.description: Otter eats urchin. Urchin eats kelp. Otters present: kelp forests thrive. Otters absent: urchin barrens. Estes & Palmisano 1974. Ecology's most famous cascade. Carbon sequestration. Biodiversity cascade.

nodes.title: Densest Fur -- 1 Million Hairs Per Square Inch
nodes.description: No blubber. Fur-only insulation. Two layers. 10-15% of day grooming. Oil vulnerability. Exxon Valdez killed ~2,800. The densest fur of any animal on Earth.

nodes.title: Mustelidae Returns -- River to Sea
nodes.description: Weasel family returns after 117 degrees. River otter (63-68, freshwater) to sea otter (185, marine). Same family, different world. Fifth marine mammal family in engine.

nodes.title: Near-Extinction and Recovery
nodes.description: 150-300K pre-exploitation to <2,000 by 1911. Fur trade. International Fur Seal Treaty (1911). ESA Threatened. WA: 59 reintroduced 1969-70, now ~2,500.

cross_references.relationship: MUSTELIDAE RETURNS! Same family, 117 degrees later. River otter (freshwater) to sea otter (marine). Weasel family from rivers to ocean.
MARINE MAMMAL ERA! Seal (Phocidae, 180) and otter (Mustelidae, 185). Different families. Same marine environment. The era continues.
MARINE MAMMALS! Sea lion (Otariidae, 182) and otter (Mustelidae, 185). Three marine mammal families in 6 degrees.
TOOL-USING TRAVELER! Zingaro carries production tools through cosmic space. Otter carries rocks in skin pouches. Both self-reliant. Both defined by their tools.
